strategy_summary: The binary is vulnerable to a buffer overflow in the vulnerable_function, which reads 512 bytes into a 128-byte buffer. We can use this to overwrite the return address and control the execution flow. Since NX is enabled, we cannot inject shellcode directly, but we can leverage ROP (Return-Oriented Programming) to call the callsystem function, which executes /bin/sh. We will need to find the offset to the return address, locate gadgets for setting up the RDI register with the address of '/bin/sh'
